ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #12, Report, Fees
Off Calendar Dec-02-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Avid Alavi for Trustee, Barbara Devries.

The Court calls the matter on for the 12th account and report of the successor trustee, and asked whether Caitlin is receiving public benefits anymore. Ms. Alavi stated Caitlin is not receiving public benefits now, that this is a long case history that pre-dates her, that there was a possible idea of termination, that she talked to Caitlin's parents yesterday who along with Barbara Devries are not in support of termination, that Caitlin stopped receiving SSI because she now has a low-paying job at a child care which pays about 35k/yr, that Caitlin did receive medical under her mother's plan until she reached the age limit, that she did receive Medi-Cal until she got medical benefits through her employment, that she is still at risk for seizures, that with current Covid situation she could get laid off and it is not easy for someone of her condition to get another job. The Court noted whether more was spent on Caitlin. Ms. Alavi stated the trust is there to enhance Caitlin since her income is not enough to support her, that the trust is here to support her and her client is willing to pay it, that Caitlin's parents worked really hard to get her where she is now and that the terms of the trust is very specific on who can terminate it.

The 12 account and report of successor trustee; etc is taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-08-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Avid Alavi, Esq. for Barbara DeVries.

The Court stated that the matter was on for the Eleventh Account and Report, that there is $150,000.00 in this trust, and that nothing was spent on the beneficiary. Ms. Alavi stated that during this period that no disbursements were needed, that Caitlin Roth has a job at Bright Horizons (pre-school), that Caitlin's parents try to encourage her independence, that Caitlin resides in low market housing, and that she takes public transportation. Ms. Alavi also stated that some tutoring was paid for which will be in the next accounting, that it's anticipated that during the next account period that Caitlin will take some courses, that some funds will be used to pay for Caitlin's courses, and that Caitlin's parents have spoken with their public benefits specialist. The Court inquired about the types of courses that will be taken by Caitlin. Ms. Alavi stated that the courses will be infant and toddler development classes that will be associated to Caitlin's job. At the conclusion of Ms. Alavi's comments, the Court granted the petition (subject to review of the order) with the expectation that it will see something used for Caitlin's benefit in the future. If not, the Court will expect something to indicate how long the Special Needs Trust will be needed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy K. Chan. Matter Not Reported.
